topic: Aspects of Hispanic Society: Modern and Traditional Values
subject: spanish

items.q: How do you describe weather during a past holiday: 'It was sunny'?
items.why: Weather backdrops in past narratives use IMPERFECT. Preterite implies a single bounded event. 'Cuando llegué, hacía sol' = ongoing weather; llegué = completed action. Standard pattern for holiday writing.

items.q: What is the gender of 'el día' (day)?
items.why: Students rely on '-a = feminine'. Common exceptions: el día, el mapa, el problema, el sistema, el clima (many Greek-origin -ma words). These appear constantly so must be memorised.

A notorious faux ami that causes real confusion. 'Estoy embarazada' = 'I am pregnant'. For 'embarrassed' use 'avergonzado/a'. Native speakers laugh at this one - learners avoid by association with shame.

items.q: How do you say 'I am browsing the internet'?
items.why: Correct answer: estoy navegando por internet. “navego por internet” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: digital divide. “digital revolution” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: In what year did the Spanish remote work law come into force?
items.why: The real figure is 2020. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
